title: Xtra Charred Vietnamese Leek Pho
description: A light, midnight-craving pho with charred leeks, fresh herbs, and a low-calorie broth.
instructions: Char the leek slices on a hot grill until lightly blackened.
Simmer the broth with garlic, ginger, fish sauce, and sugar until fragrant, about 10 minutes.
Add the charred leeks to the broth and simmer for 2 minutes to infuse flavor.
Cook the rice noodles in boiling water for 3 minutes, then drain and set aside.
Place the sliced beef in the hot broth for 30 seconds until just pink.
Assemble the pho by layering noodles, beef, and leeks in a bowl.
Pour the hot broth over the ingredients to create a steaming pot of pho.
Garnish with cilantro, mint, basil, lime wedges, and optional jalapeño slices.
Serve immediately, letting diners squeeze fresh lime juice and add more herbs as desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
